title: Revoke or replace a transfer or refund relationship
preconditions: The relationship and its transactions exist; the agent can retrieve active/retired relationship history; the owner supplies an attributable reason and idempotency identity.
postconditions: No dangling or edited-in-place link remains; every active role and amount-conservation rule holds; only active relationships affect External Spend and Budget Actual; every revocation/replacement is attributable.
main_flow: 1. The agent retrieves the active relationship, participants, all active roles, cumulative refund amounts, current spend effect, and supplies an idempotency identity. 2. The owner requests revocation or replacement with a reason. 3. Tally validates the current relationship and revalidates proposed participants against lifecycle, role exclusivity, cardinality, amount conservation, account, currency, and sign rules. 4. Tally atomically retires the old relationship with actor/reason. 5. For replacement, Tally creates one distinct valid active relationship and never edits the old link in place. 6. Active queries remove the retired effect and apply only the replacement if created. 7. History exposes the complete chain and the result reports the before/after spend effect.
alt_flows: 2a. Reason or idempotency identity is absent -> reject before mutation
3a. Relationship is already retired by the same semantically identical action -> return the original idempotent result
3b. Proposed replacement violates lifecycle, role, cardinality, amount, account, currency, or sign rules -> reject without retiring the current link
4a. Retirement and replacement cannot complete atomically -> preserve the original active relationship
6a. Recalculated named aggregates do not reconcile -> fail closed and retain the prior relationship state
metadata: {"depth_tier":3,"primary_actor":"P1","trigger":"The owner discovers that an active transfer or refund/reversal link is wrong or must point to different active transactions.","status":"draft"}
